techniques.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
techniques.evidence_quote: oai-reverse-proxy is configured by default to not use an access key if Invocation logging (prompt logging) is enabled
hijacking victim GenAI infrastructure to power their own LLM applications ... pass all the cost and compute to victim organizations

attack_chain.description: Permiso researchers placed an exposed AWS access key and secret in a file on GitHub to simulate real-world incidents where attackers obtain leaked long-lived AKIA keys.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Permiso team placed an exposed access key and secret in a file on GitHub to simulate exactly what occurred at previous clients

evidence_chunks.quote: Permiso team placed an exposed access key and secret in a file on GitHub to simulate exactly what occurred at previous clients
Permiso team placed an exposed access key and secret in a file on GitHub to simulate exactly what occurred at previous clients

attack_chain.description: Within minutes, attackers discovered and used the exposed access key to authenticate to AWS.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Within minutes actors were attempting to leverage the exposed key to perform activities in bedrock

evidence_chunks.quote: Within minutes actors were attempting to leverage the exposed key to perform activities in bedrock
Within minutes actors were attempting to leverage the exposed key to perform activities in bedrock

attack_chain.description: Attackers enumerated Bedrock foundation model availability via GetFoundationModelAvailability, ListFoundationModels, and trial InvokeModel calls to determine which models could be used.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: GetFoundationModelAvailability ... This API will return if the model is available and usable

attack_chain.description: Attackers called GetModelInvocationLoggingConfiguration to determine whether prompt/invocation logging was enabled in the account, to avoid logged environments.

attack_chain.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
attack_chain.description: Reverse-proxy tooling (e.g., oai-reverse-proxy) used by attackers automatically avoids keys where invocation logging is enabled, evading prompt logging / detection.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: oai-reverse-proxy is configured by default to not use an access key if Invocation logging (prompt logging) is enabled

attack_chain.description: Attackers programmatically submitted a use-case form (PutUseCaseForModelAccess / GetUseCaseForModelAccess), created foundation model agreements (CreateFoundationModelAgreement), and issued entitlement requests (PutFoundationModelEntitlement) to grant the compromised identity access to Anthropic Claude models in Bedrock. The use case form often listed 'smiles[.]com[.]br' as the CompanyWebsite.

Attackers invoked Anthropic Claude models in Bedrock at scale (InvokeModel and InvokeModelWithResponseStream), running over 75,000 successful model invocations in two days to power an external sexual roleplay chatbot service, passing compute costs to the victim.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Over the course of two (2) days we saw over 75,000 successful model invocations ... The scale of one actors InvokeModel, and InvokeModelWithResponseStream API calls increased dramatically

attack_chain.description: AWS Security identified the compromised key, applied the AWSCompromisedKeyQuarantineV2 policy, and eventually blocked Bedrock usage roughly 35 hours after invocations spiked; sustained attacker access was 42 days before mitigation.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: AWS-Security team did identify the compromised access key, and malicious usage of bedrock which they proactively blocked about 35 hours after the scale of invoke attempts increased into the thousands

initial_access_vector: Long-lived AWS access key (AKIA) exposed in a file on GitHub, discovered and used by attackers within minutes to access AWS Bedrock.
persistence_mechanism: Continued abuse of the valid compromised AKIA key until AWS quarantined it; attackers manipulated Bedrock model access (use case form, model agreements, entitlements) to grant themselves access to Anthropic Claude models.
privilege_escalation: Used non-console Bedrock APIs (PutUseCaseForModelAccess, CreateFoundationModelAgreement, PutFoundationModelEntitlement) to obtain foundation model access/entitlements not previously granted to the compromised identity.
